content:
I agree with the comment on Alien Crypsela - I think it's likely meant to be "Cypsela" rather than "Crypsela"? I'm not particularly educated on scientific names or flora, but everything I can find points to the correct naming being cypsela, with crypsela seeming to only show up as a misspelling...that said I'm unsure if this is intentional or if there's some additional intention behind it, so if the writer meant it to be Cryp or if there's a reasoning behind it then it makes sense to keep it that way

I agree Propagation: Caro sounds better than Propagate: Caro. Caro: Propagation doesn't work because both of the other pre-existing JP realm terminologies are of Name: Realm format (Divine Realm: Aequor and Singularity: Ultra) so it should be Propagation: Caro to match

That said, as a somewhat unrelated side-note that might be a good thing to raise with the staff - is it really a good idea to give the realm names character-specific names? Given the release of GMurphy > Vortice > Arachne > Saya as all having JP-style realm mechanics, it kind of seems like the developers are establishing a regular pattern going forward where they want to design more characters around the modified realms. That makes sense from a gameplay perspective, but lorewise I think if every new ultra character is named Singularity and every new caro character is named Propagation, it's going to seem really really weird when their lore/designs are unrelated to that concept. We kind of see a disconnect already since Vortice was given Divine Realm: Aequor and that naming makes zero sense for her in context because it was created for GMurphy's lore. It's nothing more than a suggestion since we don't have any control over this as localizers, but it might be a good idea to check with the devs to consider splitting up the lore name and the gameplay mechanic if the developers intend to design more modified realm characters going forwards...?
Chiming in as a 2nd voice that agrees with everything said here
Those two were mine, glad they helped! (kirill polivko is Riddle, just for communication purposes)
